15m in height |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}" by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, a prince of the {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. To the north lies the {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}, and to the west is the {{ill|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}}. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=draft}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Masuyama Kofun (桝山古墳) |image = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 29.47 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 46|coordinates = 42.24 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}" by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, a prince of the {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. To the north lies the {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}, and to the west is the {{ill|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}}. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=draft}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Masuyama Kofun (桝山古墳) |image = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 29.47 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 46|coordinates = 42.24 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}" by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, a prince of the {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. To the north lies the {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}, and to the west is the {{ill|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}}. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=draft}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Masuyama Kofun (桝山古墳) |image = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 28|coordinates = 29.47 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 46|coordinates = 42.24 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = top }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, Toya-cho, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}" by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}, a prince of the {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. To the north lies the {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}, and to the west is the {{ill|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}}. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=draft}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=draft}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - Kashihara City Tourism [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Masuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Masuyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Masuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Masuyama Kofun]] [[en:Masuyama Kofun]] [[ja:桝山古墳]] {{citation needed|Masuyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:桝山古墳 up to rev 93995770
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 桝山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
左に墳丘、右に付加前方部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 29.47 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 42.24 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺85m
高さ15m |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[倭彦命]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「身狭桃花鳥坂墓」 |特記事項 = 方墳としては全国第1位の規模 |地図 = Japan Nara |ラベル = 桝山古墳 |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[倭彦命]]身狭桃花鳥坂墓 拝所}}]] '''桝山古墳'''（ますやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町にある古墳。形状は[[方墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''身狭桃花鳥坂墓'''（むさのつきさかのはか、身狹桃花鳥坂墓）」として第10代[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]の[[倭彦命]]の墓に治定されている。 方墳としては全国で最大規模の古墳である。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南縁、貝吹山北麓の尾根先端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。「桝山（ますやま）」の古墳名は、墳丘が方丘であることによる{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の皇族墓として同庁の管理下にある。 墳形は元々は方形であるが、後世に北東部に前方部が付加されて前方後円形に生垣が巡らされている{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。一辺約85メートル{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または約90メートル[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html 桝山古墳（崇神天皇皇子倭彦命墓）]（橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。）・高さ約15メートルを測るが、これは方墳としては全国で最大規模になる{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]片が検出されている{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。その他については詳らかでない。 この桝山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃（または5世紀末-[[6世紀]]前半頃{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の築造と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は宮内庁により[[倭彦命]]の墓に治定されている{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。本古墳の北方には[[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[宣化天皇]] 身狭桃花鳥坂上陵）が、西方には[[新沢千塚古墳群]]があり、それらとの関係性が指摘される{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 被葬者 == 桝山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第10代[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]の'''[[倭彦命]]'''（やまとひこのみこと）の墓に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）8コマ。{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 400。。倭彦命について、『日本書紀』では垂仁天皇28年に薨去して「身狭桃花鳥坂（むさのつきさか）」に葬られたとし、その際の殉死の悲惨な様によって殉死の禁令が出されたとする（埴輪起源説話の発端）{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵寮]]では記載を欠く{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}。 その後、倭彦命墓の所在に関する所伝は失われ、[[江戸時代]]には『大和志』・『大和名所図会』等において[[鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠]]東方の石室を倭彦命墓に比定する説もあった{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}。[[1877年]]（[[明治]]10年）4月に[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]によって現在の墓に定められ、[[1886年]]（明治19年）に宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）によって用地買収とともに同地にあった神社が移転され、[[1890年]]（明治23年）から修営された{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}。ただしその治定には否定的な見解が強い{{Sfn|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石井茂輔|year=|chapter=身狭桃花鳥坂墓|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=桝山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|桝山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=大塚初重|authorlink=大塚初重|year=1989|chapter=桝山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|桝山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭彦命]] * [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html 桝山古墳（崇神天皇皇子倭彦命墓）] - 橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」 {{デフォルトソート:ますやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Masuyama Kofun]][[ja:桝山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48744177]] Wikidata name: Masuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|34.474853|N|135.7784|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka [[sn:commons:Category:Masuyama Kofun]][[ja:桝山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48744177]] Wikidata name: Masuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Masuyama Kofun (桝山古墳) |image = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|34.474853|N|135.7784|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - 15m in height |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - 15m in height |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - [[sn:commons:Category:Masuyama Kofun]][[ja:桝山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48744177]] Wikidata name: Masuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Masuyama Kofun (桝山古墳) |image = [[File:Masuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound on the left, added front section on the right |Other name = | part_of = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|34.474853|N|135.7784|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 85m per side
15m in height |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial Chamber = Unknown |Buried Person = (As determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misano Tsukisaka no Haka" (身狭桃花鳥坂墓) |notes = Largest square Kofun in Japan |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Masuyama Kofun |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label_Position = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Masuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}]] '''Masuyama Kofun''' (桝山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], Toya-cho, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. It is the largest square Kofun in Japan. == Overview == The Masuyama Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Kaibuki Mountain, near the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The mound's name derives from its rectangular type. Currently managed by the Imperial Household Agency, the site is preserved as a Historic Site1 imperial burial site. Originally, the mound was a simple square type, but a front projection was later added to the northeast side, giving it a keyhole-like appearance. The mound measures approximately 85 meters per side and 15 meters in height, making it the largest square kofun in Japan. The mound is composed of three tiers. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. Archaeological studies date the kofun to the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], or possibly between the late 5th and early 6th centuries. While the actual occupant is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has Historic Site1 the site as the burial place of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of the [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty. The exact location of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto’s tomb became unclear over time. During the Edo period, theories emerged linking the tomb to nearby stone chambers such as {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita|ja|鬼の俎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}. In 1877, the Ministry of Interior Historic Site1 the current site as his mausoleum, and by 1886, the area was purchased and restored by the Imperial Household Agency. However, skepticism remains regarding the accuracy of this Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shigesuke Ishii|year=|chapter=Misano Tsukisaka no Haka|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Misano Tsukisaka no Haka}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30 Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|authorlink=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Masuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Masuyama Kofun (Kofun)}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/masumaya.html Masuyama Kofun (Tomb of Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto)] - The kofun is square-shaped and Historic Site1 as the "Imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}" by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] have been found on its surface, but further details remain unclear. To the north lies the {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|lt=Toya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]], and to the west is the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzawa Senchizuka Kofun Group]]. Their spatial relationships suggest possible cultural or ritual connections. == Buried Person == Although the actual occupant remains unidentified, the Imperial Household Agency designates the site as the tomb of '''{{ill|Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko|lt=Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|倭彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''', a son of Emperor Sujin. According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he died in the 28th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign and was interred at Misano Tsukisaka, where his burial prompted an imperial prohibition on sacrificial burials due to their cruelty.
